forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,66 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a180
+title: 'shell execute command: include last 32 output lines in default response'
+---
+## What
+
+Today the shell tool's `execute command` operation returns only status metadata ‚ÄĒ the agent must make a second `get lines` call to see any output. Make the default response also include the **last 32 lines** of the command's output, so the common case (run a command, read its tail) is a single round-trip.
+
+Implement in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/shell/execute_command/mod.rs`, in `finalize_completed` (currently lines 142‚Äď162). After `store_command_output(state, cmd_id, &output).await` persists the output, read the tail back and append it to the success response.
+
+Current response body:
+```
+command_id: {id}
+status: completed
+exit_code: {code}
+lines: {total}
+duration: {ms}ms
+```
+
+New response: keep that header, then append the tail of stored output below it, e.g.:
+```
+command_id: 1
+status: completed
+exit_code: 0
+lines: 100
+duration: 12ms
+
+output (last 32 of 100 lines):
+69: 69
+70: 70
+...
+100: 100
+```
+
+Approach:
+- Add a module constant `const DEFAULT_TAIL_LINES: usize = 32;`.
+- `total_lines` is already computed in `finalize_completed`. Compute the tail start as `let start = total_lines.saturating_sub(DEFAULT_TAIL_LINES) + 1;` (clamped so it is never 0; when `total_lines <= 32` this yields `1`, i.e. all lines).
+- Re-lock state (`let guard = state.lock().await;`) and call `guard.get_lines(cmd_id, Some(start), None)` ‚ÄĒ the same `ShellState::get_lines` (`shell/state.rs:252`) that the `get lines` op uses ‚ÄĒ to fetch the tail as `Vec<(usize, String)>`. Format each line `N: text`, mirroring the `get_lines` op's per-line format (`shell/get_lines/mod.rs:94`).
+- When `total_lines == 0` (no output), omit the output section (or emit `output: (none)`) ‚ÄĒ do not append an empty block.
+- Keep all existing metadata fields unchanged and in the same order; the tail is purely additive.
+
+Out of scope: the `finalize_timed_out` path (the process is killed and output may not be fully stored) and the error path ‚ÄĒ leave both as-is. Do not change `get lines`, `grep history`, the buffer size cap, or storage.
+
+Also update the doc comment in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/shell/mod.rs` (the `execute command` bullet, ~line 6) to note the response now includes the last 32 output lines.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `execute command` for a command producing > 32 lines returns the last 32 lines (and only those) appended to the status metadata, with line-number prefixes.
+- [ ] `execute command` for a command producing ‚Č§ 32 lines returns all of its output lines.
+- [ ] `execute command` for a command producing no output returns the status metadata with no output block (and does not error).
+- [ ] The existing metadata fields (`command_id`, `status`, `exit_code`, `lines`, `duration`) remain present and unchanged.
+- [ ] `DEFAULT_TAIL_LINES` is a named constant set to 32.
+
+## Tests
+Add to the `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/shell/execute_command/mod.rs`. Import `extract_text` from `super::super::test_helpers` (it is `pub(crate)`) to assert on response text, alongside the existing `TestCommandBuilder`/`ResultValidator` helpers.
+
+- [ ] `test_execute_response_includes_last_32_lines`: run `seq 1 100`, get the response text via `extract_text`; assert it contains `"100: 100"` and `"69: 69"` (line 69 = the 32nd-from-last: `100 - 32 + 1`), and does NOT contain `"68: 68"` (the 33rd-from-last, just outside the window).
+- [ ] `test_execute_response_includes_all_lines_when_short`: run `printf 'a\nb\nc\n'`; assert the response text contains `a`, `b`, and `c`.
+- [ ] `test_execute_response_no_output_section_when_empty`: run `true`; assert the response still reports `status: completed` / `exit_code: 0` and contains no `69:`-style line block (no spurious empty output section), and the call is not an error.
+- [ ] Existing `test_output_metadata_in_response` still passes (metadata header preserved).
+- [ ] Run: `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-tools execute_command` ‚ÄĒ all tests pass, including the three new ones.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` ‚ÄĒ write the three failing tests first, watch them fail (RED), then implement the tail-append in `finalize_completed` to make them pass (GREEN), then refactor.
\ No newline at end of file

@@ -60,53 +60,34 @@

-Approach:
-- Add a module constant `const DEFAULT_TAIL_LINES: usize = 32;`.
-- `total_lines` is already computed in `finalize_completed`. Compute the tail start as `let start = total_lines.saturating_sub(DEFAULT_TAIL_LINES) + 1;` (clamped so it is never 0; when `total_lines <= 32` this yields `1`, i.e. all lines).
-- Re-lock state (`let guard = state.lock().await;`) and call `guard.get_lines(cmd_id, Some(start), None)` ‚ÄĒ the same `ShellState::get_lines` (`shell/state.rs:252`) that the `get lines` op uses ‚ÄĒ to fetch the tail as `Vec<(usize, String)>`. Format each line `N: text`, mirroring the `get_lines` op's per-line format (`shell/get_lines/mod.rs:94`).
-- Choose the output-block header by whether the output was truncated, i.e. `total_lines > DEFAULT_TAIL_LINES`:
-  - Truncated ‚Üí `output (last 32 of {total} lines):` ‚ÄĒ the "last N of M" wording is the implicit hint that the rest is available via `get lines`.
-  - Full ‚Üí `output ({total} lines):` (no "last ‚Ä¶ of ‚Ä¶", no get-lines hint) ‚ÄĒ this IS the complete output.
-- When `total_lines == 0` (no output), omit the output section entirely ‚ÄĒ do not append an empty block or a header.
-- Keep all existing metadata fields unchanged and in the same order; the tail is purely additive.
-
-Out of scope: the `finalize_timed_out` path (the process is killed and output may not be fully stored) and the error path ‚ÄĒ leave both as-is. Do not change `get lines`, `grep history`, the buffer size cap, or storage.
